commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human maintainer/security review is needed for the token-destination policy and compatibility break; there is no narrow automated repair to queue.; Needs attention: The patch appears to narrow the token-destination boundary, but it changes where bearer tokens may be posted and needs maintainer/security signoff. (sha=04369ce1373b4e73982cb546b087b2393a98ec63)
commands.review_summary:
The PR origin-couples `/auth/docs` loopback return destinations to loopback app origins, removes production loopback CSP form targets, adds regression tests, and records the token-destination invariant.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main accepts loopback `return_to` origins and `/auth/docs` builds the token-return callback from that value. I did not run the full authenticated browser flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human maintainer/security review is needed for the token-destination policy and compatibility break; there is no narrow automated repair to queue.

target.title: fix: restrict docs auth return origins to dev only
target.body:
## Summary

`/auth/docs` (Ask Molty) signs a user in and auto-POSTs their ClawHub auth token to the `return_to` origin. The allowlist trusted `http://localhost:4173` / `http://127.0.0.1:4173` unconditionally, so in production a user lured to `/auth/docs?return_to=http://localhost:4173/...` could have their bearer token POSTed to whatever local process held that port. This is the `/auth/docs` half of **GHSA-mp85-24m3-52jg**.

The fix couples loopback trust to the app's own origin: a loopback `return_to` is allowed only when the app itself is served from loopback, so public/staging/preview deployments never hand the token to localhost.

## What Changed

- `src/lib/docsAuth.ts` replaces the flat allowlist with origin-coupling. Production docs origins (`clawhub.ai`, `documentation.openclaw.ai`, `docs.openclaw.ai`) are always allowed; a loopback `return_to` is allowed only when `currentOrigin` is also loopback. Server render has no browser origin, so loopback is rejected there too.
- `vercel.json` removes loopback origins from production CSP `form-action` and keeps browser-side POST policy aligned with the app allowlist: `'self'`, `https://clawhub.ai`, `https://documentation.openclaw.ai`, and `https://docs.openclaw.ai`.
- `specs/auth-identity.md` records the docs-auth token-destination invariant and the CSP alignment requirement.
- `src/lib/docsAuth.test.ts` adds regression coverage for the docs-auth matrix below.

This restores the reviewed origin-coupled implementation that briefly existed on main (`e86aa30a`) before the docs-subdomain move reverted it, and re-adds `https://clawhub.ai`.

## Flow

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  subgraph After["AFTER - origin-coupled"]
    A2[return_to] --> B2{production docs origin?}
    B2 -- yes --> OK2[ALLOW]
    B2 -- no --> C2{return_to loopback?}
    C2 -- no --> NO2[REJECT]
    C2 -- yes --> D2{app origin loopback?}
    D2 -- yes --> OKL[ALLOW local-to-local]
    D2 -- no --> PUB[REJECT: public app cannot POST token to localhost]
  end
  subgraph Before["BEFORE - flat allowlist"]
    A1[return_to] --> B1{in fixed set incl. localhost:4173?}
    B1 -- yes --> OK1[ALLOW, even in production]
    B1 -- no --> NO1[REJECT]
  end
```

## Behavior

| `return_to` | Before | After @ public origin | After @ loopback origin |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `http://localhost:4173/...` | ALLOWED | **REJECTED** | ALLOWED |
| `http://127.0.0.1:4173/...` | ALLOWED | **REJECTED** | ALLOWED |
| `https://docs.openclaw.ai/...` | ALLOWED | ALLOWED | ALLOWED |
| `https://clawhub.ai/...` | absent | ALLOWED | ALLOWED |
| `https://evil.example.com/...` | REJECTED | REJECTED | REJECTED |

## Runtime Proof

Playwright drove the real shipped `src/lib/docsAuth.ts` module in a browser at two origins. `normalizeDocsReturnTo` reads `window.location.origin`, so each load exercises the real decision. Only the app origin differs between runs.

Captured browser diagnostics, prod-style non-loopback app origin:

```text
App origin (window.location.origin): http://127.0.2.2:5199

return_to                         normalizeDocsReturnTo          callback URL                               verdict
http://localhost:4173/start       null                           null                                       REJECTED
http://127.0.0.1:4173/start       null                           null                                       REJECTED
https://docs.openclaw.ai/install  https://docs.openclaw.ai/install https://docs.openclaw.ai/ask-molty/auth/callback ALLOWED
https://clawhub.ai/docs/auth      https://clawhub.ai/docs/auth   https://clawhub.ai/ask-molty/auth/callback ALLOWED
https://evil.example.com/steal    null                           null                                       REJECTED
```

Captured browser diagnostics, local loopback app origin:

```text
App origin (window.location.origin): http://127.0.0.1:5199

return_to                         normalizeDocsReturnTo          callback URL                               verdict
http://localhost:4173/start       http://localhost:4173/start    http://localhost:4173/ask-molty/auth/callback ALLOWED
http://127.0.0.1:4173/start       http://127.0.0.1:4173/start    http://127.0.0.1:4173/ask-molty/auth/callback ALLOWED
https://docs.openclaw.ai/install  https://docs.openclaw.ai/install https://docs.openclaw.ai/ask-molty/auth/callback ALLOWED
https://clawhub.ai/docs/auth      https://clawhub.ai/docs/auth   https://clawhub.ai/ask-molty/auth/callback ALLOWED
https://evil.example.com/steal    null                           null                                       REJECTED
```

Scope: this exercises the origin check that decides whether the token-carrying form is built. It is not the full authenticated `/auth/docs` POST because this checkout does not have a running local Convex backend for the signed-in route.

## How To Test

```text
bun install
./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/lib/docsAuth.test.ts src/routes/auth/-docs.test.tsx
GITHUB_TOKEN=$(gh auth token) bun run ci:e2e-http
bun run ci:static
```

For the browser matrix: serve `src/lib/docsAuth.ts` through Vite, open the page at `http://127.0.0.1:<port>` and `http://127.0.2.2:<port>`, then call `normalizeDocsReturnTo(returnTo)` from the loaded module.

## Verification

```text
./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/lib/docsAuth.test.ts src/routes/auth/-docs.test.tsx -> 9 passed
bunx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json                                              -> pass
GITHUB_TOKEN=$(gh auth token) bun run ci:e2e-http                                -> pass
bun run ci:static                                                                -> pass
real-browser origin matrix (Playwright, shipped module)                          -> see Runtime Proof
```

PR base: `main` at `078d1c88b879`. Current head: `04369ce1373b`. Net diff: 4 files, 95 insertions, 9 deletions.
